name: imcntr

metadata.name: imcntr
metadata.summary: A package providing an API for imaging controller based on an Arduino Nano Every to communicate via serial port.

documentation:
# imcntr

A package providing an API for imaging controller based on an Arduino Nano Every to communicate via serial port.

## Installation

```bash
$ pip install imcntr
```

## Usage

This package was developed to communicate with the controller of the neutron imaging experiment at TRIGA Mark II at Atominstitut of TU Wien. `imcntr` provides an API to access controller functionality via Python scripts. Communication is done via a serial port. The controller, and thus the package, can be used in general for imaging experiments with radiation. The controller provides a set of functions, such as moving the sample in and out of the beam, rotating the sample for tomographic experiments, and opening and closing the beam shutter. It provides an API to access controller functionality via Python scripts.

## Contributing

Interested in contributing? Check out the contributing guidelines. Please note that this project is released with a Code of Conduct. By contributing to this project, you agree to abide by its terms.

## License

`imcntr` was created by Clemens Trunner. It is licensed under the terms of the MIT license.

## Credits

`imcntr` was created with [`cookiecutter`](https://cookiecutter.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) and the `py-pkgs-cookiecutter` [template](https://github.com/py-pkgs/py-pkgs-cookiecutter).

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr_utils.Observer

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_utils.Observer.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Observer object with an empty list to hold observers.

This list will store dictionaries containing the target callable and its associated arguments
and keyword arguments.

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_utils.Observer.subscribe
documentation:
Subscribes a new observer to the subject. The observer is added to the list with any optional arguments
or keyword arguments provided.

```
param target

The target function or callable to be notified.

type target

callable

param args

Variable-length argument list that will be passed to the target when called.

param kwargs

Arbitrary keyword arguments to be passed to the target when called.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_utils.Observer.unsubscribe
documentation:
Unsubscribes an observer from the list. Can remove all observers, remove only observers with the exact same
parameters, or remove all observers of a specific target if `all` is set to True.

```
param target

The target observer to be removed.

type target

callable, optional

param all

A flag to indicate if all observers of the given target should be removed. Defaults to False.

type all

bool

param args

Optional arguments that must match the subscribed arguments for removal.

param kwargs

Arbitrary keyword arguments to match for removal.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_utils.Observer.call
documentation:
Calls each subscribed observer with the arguments and keyword arguments passed to `call()`.
Additionally, observers can be called with their specific arguments that were provided during subscription.

```
param args

Arguments that will be passed to each observer along with its subscribed arguments.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments that will be passed to each observer along with its subscribed kwargs.

raises RuntimeError

If an error occurs while calling the observer function.

raises TypeError

If the number of arguments provided does not match the target function's signature.
```

Implements the Observer design pattern, allowing multiple observers to subscribe to a subject
and be notified when the subject triggers an event. Observers can subscribe with additional arguments
or keyword arguments, and the `call` method allows notifying them with specific data.

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.MessageExchange

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.MessageExchange.receive
documentation:
Called when new data is available at the serial port. It subsequently calls
all the subscribed observers.

```
param data

Data received on the serial port.

type data

str
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.MessageExchange.connection_lost
documentation:
Called when the connection is closed. It subsequently calls all the subscribed
observers for connection loss.

```
param e

The exception that caused the connection to be lost.

type e

Exception
```

Expands {class}`SerialCommunication` with observers to be called when data is received
or connection is lost.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the parent  class.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the parent  class.
```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.WaitForMessage

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.WaitForMessage.wait
documentation:
Blocks until the expected message is received. If no timeout is passed, the
instance timeout is used.

```
param timeout

Time in seconds to wait for the message. Defaults to None.

type timeout

float, optional

raise RuntimeError

If a timeout occurs before receiving the expected message.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.WaitForMessage._receive_message
documentation:
Called by the receive observer when data is received.

```
param data

Data received from the controller.

type data

str
```

Provides the ability to wait for an incoming message from the connected controller
until a timeout occurs.

```
param protocol

Instance of  with an open connection.

type protocol



param message

Incoming message to be waited for.

type message

str

param timeout

Timeout in seconds to wait, defaults to None.

type timeout

float, optional
```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.SendMessage

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_communication.SendMessage.__call__
documentation:
Sends the command to the controller via the protocol.

```
raises RuntimeError

If sending the message fails.
```

Expands {class}`WaitForMessage` with functionality for sending a defined command by
calling the instance.

```
param protocol

Instance of  with an open connection.

type protocol



param message

Incoming message to be waited for.

type message

str

param command

Command to be sent to the controller.

type command

str
```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the SerialCommunication instance with an optional serial port.

```
param port

The serial port to connect to (optional).

type port

str, optional
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.connection
documentation:
Returns the serial connection instance and the associated thread.

By setting this property, the serial port for the connection is applied.

```
return

The serial connection and thread instance.

rtype

tuple
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.connection
documentation:
Sets the serial port for the connection.

```
param port

The port to use for the serial connection.

type port

str
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.connect
documentation:
Establishes the serial connection by opening the connection and starting the reader thread for reading and writing lines.
Raises a {exc}`RuntimeError` if the connection fails.

This method also prepares the connection for handling incoming data and managing the communication thread.

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication._connect_to_serial_port
documentation:
Opens the serial connection and handles any exceptions that might occur during the process.

```
raises RuntimeError

If the connection cannot be established due to various exceptions (ValueError, SerialException, etc.)
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication._start_serial_reader_thread
documentation:
Starts the serial read/write thread and waits for the connection to establish.
It overrides the `handle_line` method from the {class}`LineReader` class with the {meth}`receive` method
to provide the ability to modify behavior when a new line is received.

```
raises RuntimeError

If starting the thread fails.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.disconnect
documentation:
Terminates the serial connection by stopping the reader thread and closing the connection.

```
raises RuntimeError

If the connection could not be closed properly.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.connected
documentation:
Checks if the serial connection and the reader thread are both active.

```
return

True if the connection is open and the thread is alive, False otherwise.

rtype

bool
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.send
documentation:
Sends data to the serial port. If sending fails, raises a {exc}`RuntimeError`.

```
param text

The message to be written to the serial port.

type text

str

raises RuntimeError

If writing to the serial port fails.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.receive
documentation:
Called when data is received on the serial port. This method should be overridden by subclasses.

```
param data

The data received from the serial port.

type data

str

raises NotImplementedError

If the method is not overridden by a subclass.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.connection_lost
documentation:
Called when the connection is closed. If the cause was an error, it is re-raised as {exc}`RuntimeError`.

```
param e

The exception that caused the connection loss.

type e

Exception

raises RuntimeError

If an exception caused the connection loss.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.__enter__
documentation:
Enter the context manager, establishes the serial connection, and raises {exc}`RuntimeError` if the connection fails.

```
raises RuntimeError

If the connection could not be opened.
```

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr_connection.SerialCommunication.__exit__
documentation:
Exit the context manager, disconnect from the serial port.

This method ensures that the connection is closed when exiting the context.

```
param type

The exception type, if any.

param value

The exception value, if any.

param traceback

The traceback object, if any.
```

Provides tools for serial communication with the {mod}`serial.threaded` module to maintain a connection to an Arduino controller over the serial port.
It also provides functionality to be used in a context manager.

This class can be used to manage a serial connection and facilitate communication by sending and receiving data through the serial port.

:::{seealso}
\<<https://pyserial.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pyserial_api.html#module-serial.threaded>>
:::

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr._Advanced_Wait

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr._Advanced_Wait.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the wait instance, setting the expected message.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the parent .

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the parent .
```

A base class for waiting for a specific message from the controller. This class
extends {class}`WaitForMessage` and simplifies the usage by setting the expected message as a class constant.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the parent  class.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the parent  class.

ivar _EXPECTED_MESSAGE

The message to wait for.

type _EXPECTED_MESSAGE

str or None
```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr._Advanced_Command

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr._Advanced_Command.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the command instance, setting the outgoing message and expected response.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the parent .

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the parent .
```

A base class for sending commands to the controller and waiting for a response.
This class extends {class}`SendMessage` and simplifies usage by setting the outgoing command and expected response message as class constants.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the parent  class.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the parent  class.

ivar _OUTGOING_MESSAGE

The outgoing message to send to the controller.

type _OUTGOING_MESSAGE

str

ivar _EXPECTED_MESSAGE

The expected response from the controller after sending the command.

type _EXPECTED_MESSAGE

str
```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr.Clockwise

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr.Clockwise.__call__
documentation:
Adds the number of steps to the rotation command and sends it to the controller.

```
param steps

The number of steps to rotate the sample clockwise.

type steps

int
```

Sends the `"rot_cw+STEPS"` command to rotate the sample clockwise by the given number
of steps. Waits for the response `"rot_stopped"` after the rotation is finished.

```
param args

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the parent  class.

ivar _OUTGOING_MESSAGE

The outgoing command ().

type _OUTGOING_MESSAGE

str

ivar _EXPECTED_MESSAGE

The expected response ().

type _EXPECTED_MESSAGE

str
```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr.Controller

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr.Controller.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the controller and checks readiness and connection.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the  and  classes.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the  and  classes.
```

Provides methods to interact with the controller, such as checking if it's ready and connected.

```

The instance for checking if the controller is ready.

type ready



ivar connected

The instance for checking if the controller is connected.

```

classes.name: imcntr.imcntr.Sample

methods.name: imcntr.imcntr.Sample.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the sample and provides movement and rotation functionality.

```
param args

Arguments passed to the respective command classes.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments passed to the respective command classes.
```

Provides methods to move the sample in or out, rotate, and stop movements.

```
ivar move_out

The instance for moving the sample out.

type move_out



ivar move_in

The instance for moving the sample in.

type move_in



ivar move_stop

The instance for stopping linear movement.

type move_stop



ivar rotate_cw

The instance for rotating the sample clockwise.

type rotate_cw



ivar rotate_ccw

The instance for rotating the sample counterclockwise.

type rotate_ccw



ivar rotate_stop

The instance for stopping rotational movement.

type rotate_stop



ivar stop

The instance for stopping all movements.

type stop

```
